segments.text: Zedekiah son of Josiah was made king of Judah by Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon; he reigned in place of Jehoiachin son of Jehoiakim.
Neither he nor his attendants nor the people of the land paid any attention to the words the Lord had spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
King Zedekiah, however, sent Jehukal son of Shelemiah with the priest Zephaniah son of Maaseiah to Jeremiah the prophet with this message:
Please pray to the Lord our God for us.
segments.speaker: King Zedekiah

segments.text: Now Jeremiah was free to come and go among the people, for he had not yet been put in prison.
Pharaoh's army had marched out of Egypt, and when the Babylonians who were besieging Jerusalem heard the report about them, they withdrew from Jerusalem.
Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah the prophet:
Tell the king of Judah, who sent you to inquire of me, 'Pharaoh's army, which has marched out to support you, will go back to its own land, to Egypt.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then the Babylonians will return and attack this city; they will capture it and burn it down.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Do not deceive yourselves, thinking, 'The Babylonians will surely leave us.' They will not!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Even if you were to defeat the entire Babylonian army that is attacking you and only wounded men were left in their tents, they would come out and burn this city down.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: After the Babylonian army had withdrawn from Jerusalem because of Pharaoh's army,
Jeremiah started to leave the city to go to the territory of Benjamin to get his share of the property among the people there.
But when he reached the Benjamin Gate, the captain of the guard, whose name was Irijah son of Shelemiah, the son of Hananiah, arrested him and said,
You are deserting to the Babylonians!
segments.speaker: Irijah

segments.text: Jeremiah said,
That's not true! I am not deserting to the Babylonians.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: But Irijah would not listen to him; instead, he arrested Jeremiah and brought him to the officials.
They were angry with Jeremiah and had him beaten and imprisoned in the house of Jonathan the secretary, which they had made into a prison.
Jeremiah was put into a vaulted cell in a dungeon, where he remained a long time.
Then King Zedekiah sent for him and had him brought to the palace, where he asked him privately,
Is there any word from the Lord?
segments.speaker: King Zedekiah

segments.text: Yes, you will be delivered into the hands of the king of Babylon.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: What crime have I committed against you or your attendants or this people, that you have put me in prison?
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Where are your prophets who prophesied to you, 'The king of Babylon will not attack you or this land'?
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: But now, my lord the king, please listen. Let me bring my petition before you: Do not send me back to the house of Jonathan the secretary, or I will die there.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: King Zedekiah then gave orders for Jeremiah to be placed in the courtyard of the guard and given a loaf of bread from the street of the bakers each day until all the bread in the city was gone. So Jeremiah remained in the courtyard of the guard.
